predictions.text: The EU will adopt a new Iran-related restrictive-measures package adding at least 20 listings (persons and/or entities) in a single Council Decision/Implementing Regulation.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by the deadline, the EU publishes in the Official Journal (EUR-Lex) a Council Implementing Decision and/or Implementing Regulation that (a) explicitly concerns Iran-related sanctions/restrictive measures and (b) adds >=20 new listings in that single legal act (counting persons and entities added).
evidence: Recent reporting highlights a significant U.S.–Israel–Iran escalation and widening economic pressure tools being used alongside military activity.
https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/02/us/politics/trump-war-iran-israel.html
monitoring_signals: EU Council press releases on restrictive measures related to Iran
EUR-Lex updates to Iran sanctions regimes and annexes
Statements from major EU member-state foreign ministries on Iran sanctions coordination
predictions.rationale: Escalation tends to trigger coordinated EU measures; threshold set high (20 listings) to make verification unambiguous.

predictions.text: The U.S. Department of Energy will announce an SPR release (sale or exchange) totaling at least 10 million barrels, explicitly citing Middle East conflict/escalation as a primary driver.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if DOE issues an official press release (or publishes a notice) by the deadline announcing an SPR crude-oil sale and/or exchange with total volume >=10,000,000 barrels and the announcement explicitly references Middle East conflict/escalation (e.g., Iran/Strait of Hormuz) as a key justification.
evidence: Strait of Hormuz disruption risk is being discussed as a tail risk with potential oil-supply consequences.
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/02/strait-of-hormuz-crisis-us-iran-israel-war-shipping-trade-oil.html
monitoring_signals: DOE SPR press releases and sale/exchange announcements
White House statements linking gasoline/oil prices to Middle East disruption
Weekly EIA petroleum status reports showing SPR drawdowns paired with announced actions
predictions.rationale: SPR actions are plausible if retail fuel inflation becomes salient, but policy reluctance and timing uncertainty keep probability moderate.

predictions.text: Iran will detain, seize, or forcibly divert at least one non-Iranian-flagged commercial oil/product tanker in/near the Strait of Hormuz, with the detention lasting at least 24 hours.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by the deadline, a major wire/service (e.g., Reuters/AP/AFP) reports Iran’s forces detain/seize/divert a non-Iranian-flagged oil/product tanker in/near the Strait of Hormuz (or adjacent Persian Gulf waters) and the report indicates the vessel was held (not merely approached/hailed) for >=24 hours, corroborated by official statements (flag state/ship operator) or maritime security advisories (e.g., UKMTO/IMB).
evidence: Heightened conflict increases incentives for maritime coercion and raises Hormuz disruption concerns.
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/02/strait-of-hormuz-crisis-us-iran-israel-war-shipping-trade-oil.html
monitoring_signals: UKMTO incident reports and advisories
IMB piracy/incident bulletins
AIS anomalies and shipping security updates (Lloyd’s List, TradeWinds, Reuters shipping)
predictions.rationale: Iran has precedent for tanker detentions; escalation raises risk, but not all harassment crosses the detention/seizure threshold.

predictions.text: Dubai International Airport (DXB) or Hamad International Airport (DOH) will suspend commercial passenger departures and arrivals for at least 12 consecutive hours due to regional conflict/security risk.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by the deadline, DXB or DOH has a documented continuous closure/suspension of commercial passenger operations lasting >=12 hours, confirmed by an official airport operator statement, civil aviation authority notice, or widely cited NOTAM-based reporting, with the cause attributed to conflict/security risk in the region.
evidence: Reporting notes Iran-linked strikes affecting aviation and corporate operations in the UAE/Bahrain region.
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/02/amazon-says-drone-strikes-damaged-3-facilities-in-uae-and-bahrain.html
monitoring_signals: Dubai Airports / Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announcements
NOTAM feeds and aviation-industry reporting on airspace/airport disruptions
Carrier operational bulletins citing airport closure (not just schedule reductions)
predictions.rationale: Airspace risk is elevated, but Gulf hubs are resilient and may route around disruption without full closures.

predictions.text: AAA’s U.S. national average retail gasoline price will exceed $4.25 per gallon on at least one day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if AAA’s Daily Fuel Gauge Report (national average regular gasoline) shows a value strictly greater than $4.25/gal for any date on or before the deadline.
evidence: Oil rally and Middle East risk premium are being discussed as drivers of inflation pass-through fears.
https://www.marketwatch.com/story/chevrons-stock-surges-toward-a-record-as-u-s-israeli-attacks-on-iran-spark-an-oil-rally-c6283fd5
monitoring_signals: AAA daily national gasoline price series
EIA weekly retail gasoline prices and refinery utilization
WTI/Brent spot and front-month moves tied to Hormuz risk headlines
predictions.rationale: Retail gasoline can lag crude; threshold is high but reachable if crude stays elevated and refining margins widen.

predictions.text: From the 2026-03-03 close to the 2026-06-30 close, XLE will outperform SPY by at least 10 percentage points using adjusted-close total returns.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if (XLE adjusted close / XLE adjusted close on 2026-03-03 − 1) − (SPY adjusted close / SPY adjusted close on 2026-03-03 − 1) >= 0.10 using a single public data source (e.g., Nasdaq or Yahoo Finance) for adjusted closes on the specified dates.
evidence: Oil rally has already lifted major energy equities, indicating market sensitivity to geopolitics-driven crude moves.
https://www.marketwatch.com/story/chevrons-stock-surges-toward-a-record-as-u-s-israeli-attacks-on-iran-spark-an-oil-rally-c6283fd5
monitoring_signals: Relative performance of XLE vs SPY during crude spikes
Earnings guidance revisions for integrated oil majors
Backwardation/contango shifts in crude curves signaling sustained vs transient risk premium
predictions.rationale: If conflict risk persists, energy equities can continue to lead; but mean reversion and broad-market risk-off could cap relative gains.

predictions.text: Amazon will disclose charges and/or expected costs of at least $250 million attributable to the UAE/Bahrain facility damage from the reported drone strikes.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an Amazon SEC filing (10-Q, 10-K, or 8-K) or official earnings materials by the deadline explicitly quantify charges/costs (impairment, repairs, business interruption, security, etc.) linked to the UAE/Bahrain drone-strike damage totaling >=$250,000,000 (single figure or sum of clearly delineated figures).
evidence: Amazon reported damaged facilities in UAE and Bahrain from drone strikes, creating potential for material disclosed costs.
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/02/amazon-says-drone-strikes-damaged-3-facilities-in-uae-and-bahrain.html
monitoring_signals: Amazon 10-Q/8-K risk-factor updates referencing Middle East operations
Earnings-call Q&A on regional operational disruption
Insurance recovery disclosures and impairment/asset write-down line items
predictions.rationale: Materiality threshold is high; Amazon may absorb/insure costs without itemized disclosure.

predictions.text: Nvidia will publicly launch or announce a data-center networking product/platform explicitly described by Nvidia as using “silicon photonics” or “co-packaged optics.”
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by the deadline, Nvidia issues a press release, product page, or official keynote material announcing a data-center networking product/platform and the official description includes the exact phrase “silicon photonics” or “co-packaged optics.”
evidence: Nvidia’s reported investment into photonics suppliers signals strategic emphasis on optical interconnect bottlenecks.
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/02/nvidia-investment-coherent-lumentum.html
monitoring_signals: Nvidia GTC/keynote agendas and networking announcements
Supplier updates from major photonics firms naming Nvidia programs
Industry reporting on CPO/optical interconnect roadmaps tied to Nvidia platforms
predictions.rationale: Optical scaling pressures make a labeled silicon-photonics/CPO product plausible within two quarters, but naming/positioning risk remains.

predictions.text: CDC will report at least 1,000 U.S. measles cases (confirmed + probable, cumulative) for 2026 year-to-date in its official surveillance totals.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if CDC’s official measles surveillance (weekly or periodic national totals) shows cumulative U.S. cases in calendar year 2026 reaching >=1,000 on or before the deadline (using CDC-reported confirmed/probable definitions).
evidence: Public-health signals are described as worsening, with measles status discussions delayed amid broader health-system strain narratives.
https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/02/health/hiv-drugs-ryan-white.html
monitoring_signals: CDC measles updates and weekly case totals
State health department outbreak declarations and school exposure notices
Vaccination coverage and exemption-rate changes in large states
predictions.rationale: 1,000+ is an extreme but historically precedented U.S. level; outbreaks can accelerate, but containment often prevents reaching this threshold.

predictions.text: New York State will enact (governor signs) a new congressional district map to be used for the 2026 U.S. House elections, replacing the prior map.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, by the deadline, New York enacts a law (bill signed) adopting a new congressional district plan for the 2026 elections, with the enacted text or accompanying official documentation indicating it supersedes the prior congressional map (and is not merely a proposed/unpassed plan).
evidence: The Supreme Court action affecting a Republican-held NYC district increases the chance of renewed state-level redistricting attempts.
https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/02/us/politics/supreme-court-malliotakis-new-york.html
monitoring_signals: NY legislature redistricting bill introductions and committee movement
NY Court of Appeals / federal court rulings affecting NY maps
Statements from NY’s Independent Redistricting Commission and the governor’s office
predictions.rationale: Litigation and political incentives could spur another map attempt, but procedural constraints and court oversight lower the probability.
